name: Harbor Container Registry API
what_it_does: Open-source container image registry with REST API offering vulnerability scanning, content signing, replication, and RBAC for secure enterprise Kubernetes environments.
use_cases: Programmatically managing container image repositories and access control
Triggering vulnerability scans on newly pushed images via API webhooks
Querying vulnerability reports for images before Kubernetes deployment
Replicating images between environments (dev/staging/prod) via API
Managing Helm chart repositories with API-driven chart push/pull automation
not_for: Public image hosting (Harbor is designed for private enterprise use)
Organizations without Kubernetes operations team to maintain self-hosted Harbor
Simple personal Docker image storage (Docker Hub is easier)
best_when: An agent manages enterprise Kubernetes image lifecycle — scanning, promoting, and deploying container images in air-gapped or compliance-sensitive environments.
avoid_when: You need managed cloud registry without operational overhead (use AWS ECR, GHCR, or Google Artifact Registry instead).

auth.notes: Robot accounts (service accounts) for automated access with project-scoped permissions. OIDC integration for enterprise SSO. Bearer tokens for API calls. Robot accounts have granular permissions per project.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Apache 2.0 open-source: completely free, unlimited images
pricing.paid_tiers: VMware Tanzu includes Harbor support; third-party managed offerings available
pricing.notes: Harbor is CNCF graduated project — vendor-neutral open-source. Self-host on any Kubernetes cluster. No SaaS commercial version; deploy and operate yourself.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI specification is published and comprehensive. Swagger UI available at /devcenter-api-2.0. Error responses are structured JSON with HTTP status codes and messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Image push with same digest is idempotent. Project and repository creation is idempotent with name-based deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Image scanning results depend on Trivy/Clair configuration — scanner must be configured and running
Webhook payloads differ by Harbor version — verify webhook format matches your version
Robot account token rotation must be managed; tokens expire based on configured duration
Replication rules have complex configuration — destination registry auth must be pre-configured

security_score_components.security_notes: CNCF security practices. Robot accounts with project-scoped permissions. Content trust (Notary) for image signing. Trivy/Clair vulnerability scanning integrated. Open-source codebase is auditable.
